meaning: n.偷懒者,懒鬼,游手好闲的人
examples: "Stand up and repeat 'TIS THE VOICE OF THE SLUGGARD,'" said the Gryphon.
As a door turns on its hinges, so a sluggard turns on his bed.
I will not like a sluggard wear out my youth in idleness at home.
If you are a sluggard in paying bills and have a low credit score, are you more likely to get into auto accidents?
What separates a growth REIT from a sluggard?
